commands.review_summary:
The branch restricts Anthropic OAuth callback hosts to loopback values, derives redirect URIs from the selected host, changes the default callback host to localhost, and adds callback-host tests.

PR surface: Source +24, Tests +20. Total +44 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main and v2026.6.10 can bind the callback server to OPENCLAW_OAUTH_CALLBACK_HOST while sending a hardcoded localhost redirect URI; I did not run a live Anthropic OAuth flow.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor code changes plus real provider-flow proof and maintainer security/compatibility acceptance, so it is not a safe repair-marker or automerge candidate right now.

target.title: fix(anthropic): restrict OAuth callback host to loopback addresses and derive redirect URI
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The Anthropic OAuth helper in `src/llm/utils/oauth/anthropic.ts` accepted any value for `OPENCLAW_OAUTH_CALLBACK_HOST`, including external addresses like `attacker.example.com`. Meanwhile, `REDIRECT_URI` was hardcoded to `http://localhost:53692/callback` regardless of the configured host. This inconsistency created two issues:

1. **Silent OAuth failure**: Setting `OPENCLAW_OAUTH_CALLBACK_HOST` to a non-localhost value (e.g. `192.168.1.100`) causes the callback server to bind on that address, but the `redirect_uri` sent to `claude.ai` is always `localhost`. The browser redirects to `localhost:53692` while the server listens elsewhere — the OAuth flow breaks silently with no clear error.
2. **Authorization code interception**: If an attacker controls `OPENCLAW_OAUTH_CALLBACK_HOST` (e.g. via compromised `.env` or environment injection), they can redirect the callback server to an attacker-controlled host like `attacker.example.com`. Combined with DNS rebinding, the authorization code could be intercepted, hijacking the session token.

## Why This Change Was Made

This follows the same loopback-whitelist pattern already used by the OpenAI OAuth helper (`extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-oauth-flow.runtime.ts:34-37`), which validates callback hosts and derives `REDIRECT_URI` from the validated host.

The default callback host was restored to `localhost` (matching both the shipped `main` behavior and the OpenAI sibling pattern), preserving the registered-looking `http://localhost:53692/callback` redirect URI that Anthropic expects. The loopback allowlist still enforces `localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, and `::1` only.

## User Impact

- The `OPENCLAW_OAUTH_CALLBACK_HOST` env var now only accepts loopback addresses: `localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, or `::1`. Any other value throws an error.
- `REDIRECT_URI` is derived from the validated callback host rather than being hardcoded.
- The default redirect URI is preserved as `http://localhost:53692/callback` (unchanged from shipped behavior).
- IPv6 loopback (`::1`) is properly wrapped in brackets for URL construction (`http://[::1]:53692/callback`).
- Four new regression tests cover: default host (`localhost`), host rejection, IPv6 bracket wrapping, and `127.0.0.1` redirect URI.
- Fixes #96485

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/llm/utils/oauth/anthropic.test.ts` — 8 passed (4 original + 4 new regression tests)
- `pnpm check:changed` — clean
- `pnpm build` — clean
- Autoreview: clean
- CI: all checks pass ✅

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Anthropic OAuth callback host restricted to loopback addresses only; redirect URI derived from validated host; default callback host preserved as `localhost` matching shipped behavior and OpenAI sibling pattern.

Real environment tested: Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, Node v24.13.1, commit `d33921f654` on branch `fix/issue-96485-oauth-callback-host`

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
# 1. Build and verify binary matches HEAD
pnpm build
pnpm openclaw --version

# 2. Run unit tests
pnpm test src/llm/utils/oauth/anthropic.test.ts

# 3. Real runtime proof — import actual source module via tsx
#    Tests 6 scenarios using the exact resolveCallbackHost /
#    resolveRedirectUri functions that execute at module load.
cat > /tmp/proof-96917.mjs << 'PROOF_EOF'
import { testing } from '<REPO>/src/llm/utils/oauth/anthropic.ts';

console.log('=== Real Behavior Proof: Anthropic OAuth Callback Host ===\n');

console.log('[1] attacker.example.com (external host) → rejected');
try { testing.resolveCallbackHost({ OPENCLAW_OAUTH_CALLBACK_HOST: 'attacker.example.com' }); }
catch (err) { console.log('  ✓', err.message); }

console.log('[2] 0.0.0.0 (non-loopback) → rejected');
try { testing.resolveCallbackHost({ OPENCLAW_OAUTH_CALLBACK_HOST: '0.0.0.0' }); }
catch (err) { console.log('  ✓', err.message); }

console.log('[3] Unset env → default localhost (matches shipped behavior)');
const h3 = testing.resolveCallbackHost({});
console.log('  ✓ callbackHost:', h3);
console.log('  ✓ redirectUri:', testing.resolveRedirectUri(h3));

console.log('[4] 127.0.0.1 (IPv4 loopback)');
const h4 = testing.resolveCallbackHost({ OPENCLAW_OAUTH_CALLBACK_HOST: '127.0.0.1' });
console.log('  ✓ redirectUri:', testing.resolveRedirectUri(h4));

console.log('[5] ::1 (IPv6 loopback, bracket wrapping)');
const h5 = testing.resolveCallbackHost({ OPENCLAW_OAUTH_CALLBACK_HOST: '::1' });
console.log('  ✓ redirectUri:', testing.resolveRedirectUri(h5));

console.log('[6] localhost (explicit)');
const h6 = testing.resolveCallbackHost({ OPENCLAW_OAUTH_CALLBACK_HOST: 'localhost' });
console.log('  ✓ redirectUri:', testing.resolveRedirectUri(h6));

console.log('\n✓ All 6 runtime proof scenarios passed.');
PROOF_EOF

GIT_HEAD=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD) pnpm exec tsx /tmp/proof-96917.mjs
```

Evidence after fix:

```console
$ pnpm openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.6.9 (d33921f)

$ pnpm test src/llm/utils/oauth/anthropic.test.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/0668000452/codes/OPENSOURCES/openclaw
 ✓ src/llm/utils/oauth/anthropic.test.ts (8 tests)
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)

$ pnpm exec tsx /tmp/proof-96917.mjs
=== Real Behavior Proof: Anthropic OAuth Callback Host ===

[1] attacker.example.com (external host) → rejected
  ✓ Anthropic OAuth callback host must be localhost, 127.0.0.1, or ::1
[2] 0.0.0.0 (non-loopback) → rejected
  ✓ Anthropic OAuth callback host must be localhost, 127.0.0.1, or ::1
[3] Unset env → default localhost (matches shipped behavior)
  ✓ callbackHost: localhost
  ✓ redirectUri: http://localhost:53692/callback
[4] 127.0.0.1 (IPv4 loopback)
  ✓ redirectUri: http://127.0.0.1:53692/callback
[5] ::1 (IPv6 loopback, bracket wrapping)
  ✓ redirectUri: http://[::1]:53692/callback
[6] localhost (explicit)
  ✓ redirectUri: http://localhost:53692/callback

✓ All 6 runtime proof scenarios passed.
```

Observed result after fix: All 6 real-runtime scenarios pass via `tsx` importing the actual source module (no mocks). External hosts (`attacker.example.com`) and non-loopback addresses (`0.0.0.0`) are rejected with a clear error. Default callback host is `localhost` producing `http://localhost:53692/callback` — matching shipped `main` and `v2026.6.10`. `127.0.0.1` and `::1` are accepted with correct redirect URI derivation, including IPv6 bracket wrapping. Binary build is clean and all 8 tests (4 original + 4 new regression) pass.

What was not tested: End-to-end OAuth dance with a real Anthropic account (requires live interactive credentials and browser). The fix follows the same loopback-whitelist pattern already shipped for OpenAI OAuth (`extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-oauth-flow.runtime.ts:34-37`). No migration needed — external hosts were never a secure configuration; they now error immediately with a clear message.
